description: Merge conflict resolved at tier ai-resolve. Branch: overstory/builder-repl-buttons/behavior-tree-ist-104d. Agent: builder-repl-buttons. Conflicting files: packages/studio-ui/src/repl/ReplTerminal.tsx.

name: pure-state-updater
description: State updater functions must be pure — no side effects (localStorage, fetch, etc.) inside setState(prev => ...). Move side effects to useEffect that depends on the state value.

name: displayState-dedup
description: BehaviourTree caches last JSON.stringify(state) per nodeId; emits state in TickTraceEvent only when serialized value changes. TickStore.getSnapshotAtTick fills missing state via getLastNodeState(nodeId, tickId) (carry-forward). Avoids wire traffic for unchanged node states.

title: WHI-10 mutable trees runtime design
rationale: Owner answered all 8 open questions from research phase

name: bidirectional-ring-buffer
description: RingBuffer unshift/unshiftMany prepend to oldest end, evict from newest end. When batch >= capacity, keeps newest capacity items (mirroring pushMany). TickStore.insertBefore uses unshiftMany; evicted are always previously-stored ticks (not batch overflows). TreeInspector.insertTicksBefore does NOT increment totalTickCount — historical loads are separate from live tick count.

name: use-tick-poller-windowed
description: useTickPoller signature: (selection, pollRateMs, windowSize, mode: 'streaming'|'windowed'). Returns {ticks, latestBackwardTicks, isLoading, error, fetchBefore, seekToRange}. streaming mode auto-polls forward; windowed mode only fetches on explicit fetchBefore/seekToRange calls. fetchBefore prepends + evicts newest; seekToRange replaces window. use-studio-controls.ts needs update when integrating (breaking API change from TickRecord[]).

content: Windowed time travel UI: StudioControls exposes tickBounds (StudioTickBounds={minTickId,maxTickId,totalCount}), onFetchTicksAround(tickId), isLoadingWindow. useTimeTravelControls accepts options.{onNeedTick,serverBounds,isLoading}. TimelinePanel uses serverBounds for scrubber min/max and shows 'Loaded: N/M ticks' text. BehaviourTreeDebugger wires: handleNeedTick→studioControls.onFetchTicksAround. use-studio-controls polls ticks.bounds every 5s and implements onFetchTicksAround via ticks.range fetch merged into extraTicks state.

useInspector bidirectional insert: tracks ingestedTickIdsRef as Set<number> (not lastIngestedTickId scalar). Forward ticks (tickId > newestStored) go via ingestTicks(). Backward ticks (tickId <= newestStored) go via inspector.insertTicks?.(records) optional-chained — no-op until data-layer builder adds TreeInspector.insertTicks().

StudioUiSettings windowed fields: windowSize (default 1000, target ticks loaded for time travel) and fetchBatchSize (default 1000, ticks per windowed request). Both persisted in localStorage via use-ui-settings.ts. SettingsPanel renders both as NumberField with min=100.

Profiler backward tick ingestion: ingestTicks() guards on lastProcessedTickId blocking historical ticks. Use ingestTicksBefore() which guards on tickContribByTick.has(tickId) instead. TreeInspector.insertTicksBefore must call profiler.ingestTicksBefore() not ingestTicks().

name: whi14-sequential-leads
description: WHI-14 extended time travel: 2-phase implementation — server retention (ticks.bounds, ticks.range, ticks.queryBefore) merged first, then UI layer (bidirectional TickStore, windowed useTickPoller, TimelinePanel window controls). Reconciliation builder needed when 2 builders have API contract mismatches.

name: no-isLoading-in-background-polls
description: fetchForward background poll must NOT call setIsLoading(true/false) — only user-initiated operations (fetchBefore, seekToRange) should toggle isLoading. Background polls toggling isLoading caused blinking hourglass + button flicker every poll cycle.

name: window-selector-no-auto-fetch
description: stepForward/stepBack in useTimeTravelControls stop at loaded-window boundary without auto-fetching; user explicitly moves window via TimelinePanel window selector slider (onSelectWindow -> onFetchTicksAround). Scrubber range now spans loaded window only; window-selector range spans full server bounds.

name: range-trimmer-popup
description: iOS-style range trimmer: WindowRangeTrimmer uses dual pointer-captured handles on a custom div track (not two HTML range inputs). fromFrac/toFrac state [0,1] mapped to tick IDs. Popup anchored via position:absolute on .bt-timeline__window-selector; button opens/closes via trimmerOpen state. onSelectRange(from,to) replaces old onSelectWindow(tickId) — caller does no range computation.

name: range-trimmer-apply-flow
description: Apply calls onSelectRange(from,to) then controls.goToTick(from) in TimelinePanel.handleTrimmerApply. onFetchTickRange sets manualWindowActive=true (blocks streaming poll overwrite); onResumeStreaming resets it when paused->live transition detected via prevModeRef in BehaviourTreeDebugger. windowSize removed from StudioUiSettings; seekToRange uses Math.min(to-from+1, 10000) dynamic limit; rolling cap uses ringBufferSize.

name: range-trimmer-draggable
description: WindowRangeTrimmer selection div: handleSelectionPointerDown uses setPointerCapture, records startFrac/startFrom/startTo, on pointermove shifts both fromFrac+toFrac by delta maintaining span width, clamps to [0,1-width]. CSS: pointer-events none→auto, cursor:grab/grabbing. In tests: must mock element.setPointerCapture=vi.fn() since jsdom lacks it.

name: seek-clears-inspector
description: useInspector detects window replacement (seekToRange) when ingested.size>0 and none of current ticks appear in ingestedTickIdsRef; calls inspector.clearTicks() before ingesting to prevent OLD+NEW accumulation in scrubber range. pausedInspector is refreshed via isLoadingWindow false-transition effect in BehaviourTreeDebugger.

name: seekGenRef-catch-guard
description: seekGenRef stale-fetch guard: fetchForward captures seekGen=seekGenRef.current before fetch; both .then() and .catch() return early if seekGenRef.current !== seekGen. seekToRange increments seekGenRef before starting its own fetch, preempting any in-flight polling fetch.

name: seekToRange-preempts-streaming
description: seekToRange uses seekGenRef generation counter to preempt in-flight fetchForward polls. seekToRange increments seekGenRef and proceeds regardless of fetchingRef.current. fetchForward captures seekGen before fetch and discards results in both .then() and .catch() if seekGenRef changed during flight.

name: windowMaxTicks-propagation
description: windowMaxTicks propagation: when onFetchTickRange(from,to) is called, store windowMaxTicks=to-from+1 in state; clear to null on onResumeStreaming. App.tsx uses windowMaxTicks??ringBufferSize for inspector maxTicks so tick store size matches selected window.

name: obo-inclusive-range
description: Inclusive tick ranges [from,to] have count = to - from + 1, not to - from. WindowRangeTrimmer rangeCount and seekToRange window sizing must both use +1 for correct inclusive count.

name: navigateToTick-no-fetch
description: TimeTravelControls exposes navigateToTick(tickId) for navigation-only (no onNeedTick side-effect), used by trimmer handleTrimmerApply to avoid double-fetch race.

name: window-load-auto-navigate
description: After isLoadingWindow transitions true→false while mode=paused, BehaviourTreeDebugger effect calls navigateToTick(inspector.getStats().oldestTickId) to auto-navigate scrubber to first tick of new window. navigateToTick is stable (useCallback with [] deps) so safe to include in effect deps.

name: live-seed-on-streaming-entry
description: On streaming mode entry, useTickPoller calls ticks.bounds for maxTickId, fetches ticks.range(maxTickId-cap+1, maxTickId) to seed window, sets afterTickIdRef=maxTickId, then starts poll interval. setIsLoading(true) during seed. Cleanup sets isLoading=false to prevent stuck state.

name: live-seed-from-latest
description: Streaming mode in useTickPoller seeds initial window from latest ticks (ticks.bounds then ticks.range for last ringBufferSize ticks) before starting forward polling, instead of from tick 0.

title: static-tt-windows
rationale: Prevents complexity from incremental append/fetchBefore in TT mode. Simplifies mental model: TT = frozen snapshot, live = always running. Eliminates class of bugs from overlapping windows and stale state.

content: TT windows are static snapshots. insertBefore/insertTicksBefore/ingestTicksBefore are removed. No incremental backward appends in TT mode — fetch a new window or clone the live buffer.

name: static-tt-impl
description: useTickPoller always streams (no mode param, no fetchBefore, no seekToRange). TT window = ttWindowTicks state in use-studio-controls. ticks = ttWindowTicks ?? pollerResult.ticks. onResumeStreaming sets ttWindowTicks=null for instant live switch. Live buffer accumulates in background during TT.

name: useInspector-forward-only-ingest
description: useInspector forward-only ingest: after static-TT refactor, diff-ingest effect calls inspector.ingestTicks(newTicks) directly — no cutoff/forwardTicks/backwardTicks split. Seek detection (all current ticks absent from ingested set) clears inspector and resets Set before ingest.

name: npc-demo-cpu-light
description: createNpcDemoTree() provides a CPU-light NPC demo (80+ nodes) as default --demo. Uses simple arithmetic only — no cpuBurst loops. --demo-cpu launches old CPU-heavy tree. Both can run simultaneously via separate startDemoAgent calls.

name: toggle-time-travel-callback-order
description: handleToggleTimeTravel must be defined before the keydown useEffect so Space handler can call it without duplicating toggle logic. Effect deps: [timeTravelControls, handleToggleTimeTravel, onTickChange].

name: mode-reset-isLoading-guard
description: useTimeTravelControls totalTicks===0 reset effect must guard on \!isLoading. During a window fetch, inspector is cleared temporarily (totalTicks→0); without guard, mode='live' override kills paused state set by navigateToTick(). Effect dep array must include isLoading.

name: validate-on-composite-base
description: BehaviourTree.validate() empty-children check lives on Composite base class. Subclasses with different arity (IfThenElse: 2-3) override and call super. Utility composites chain super.validate() then check instanceof Utility.

content: consistent-type-imports with fixStyle inline-type-imports: type-only imports use inline 'import { type Foo }' syntax, not separate 'import type { Foo }' declarations. ESLint auto-fixes all violations.
objectLiteralTypeAssertions: object literal assertions require double-cast via unknown: '({ ... } as unknown) as T' instead of '{ ... } as T'. Direct object literal assertions are forbidden.

name: isLoadingWindow-pausedInspector-guard
description: mode-change effect that clones live inspector into pausedInspector must guard on studioControls?.isLoadingWindow — skip clone while window fetch in-flight; isLoadingWindow-transition effect handles clone after load completes

name: freeze-bounds-on-trimmer-open
description: WindowRangeTrimmer receives frozen bounds (captured at open time) not live serverBounds — prevents handle flicker from background bounds polling while trimmer is open; frozenBounds cleared on close/apply
seek-detection-with-evicted-ids: useInspector's ingestedTickIdsRef must track inspector.getStoredTickIds() not ticks.map(t=>t.tickId)
resolution: Set ingestedTickIdsRef = new Set(inspector.getStoredTickIds()) after each ingest. Evicted IDs in the ticks array but not the inspector would false-positive the seek check, keeping stale live data in the inspector.

name: window-trimmer-clamp-defaults
description: WindowRangeTrimmer clamps defaultFrom/defaultTo to [minTickId,maxTickId] before computing initial fracs; totalSpan = maxTickId-minTickId+1 (inclusive) for correct 100% display when full range selected.

name: display-node-pattern
description: DisplayNote(name, text) and DisplayProgress(name, ()=>{progress,label?}) follow DisplayState exactly — extend Action, addFlags(NodeFlags.Display), return NodeResult.Succeeded, expose data via getDisplayState(). UI uses defaultName check in getNodeVisualKind() for visual kinds 'displayNote'/'displayProgress'.

name: byte-metrics-pattern
description: ByteMetricsService: in-memory ring buffer (maxSamples=1000); key=clientId:sessionId:treeId; ratePerSecond=windowBytes/(windowMs/1000); TickBatchHandler records Buffer.byteLength(JSON.stringify(message)) per tick batch; tRPC byteMetrics.query returns {samples,ratePerSecond,totalBytes}

name: plugin-protocol
description: StudioAgent plugin system: registerPlugin(plugin) before start(); plugins attach in start() via plugin.attach(sender) where sender.send(correlationId,payload) calls link.sendPluginMessage(pluginId,...); inbound PluginMessages routed by pluginId via link.onPluginMessage; plugins detach in destroy(). Agent stays fully agnostic to plugin details. MessageType.PluginMessage=7.

name: repl-plugin-arch
description: ReplPlugin implements StudioPlugin with NaCl E2E encryption. attach() generates ephemeral keypair, seals 32-byte session seed with UI's static public key, derives c2s/s2c directional keys via HKDF-SHA256, sends handshake. handleInbound decrypts with s2c key (UI to agent), sends encrypted response with c2s key (agent to UI). repl-types.ts defines StudioPlugin/PluginSender interfaces locally until builder-plugin-protocol wires them into interfaces.ts.

content: Use @noble/hashes@^1.6.x (resolved to 1.8.0) for HKDF in BT Studio core. v2.x changed subpath exports from ./hkdf to ./hkdf.js, breaking moduleResolution:bundler without .js extensions. Stick to v1.x range until a coordinated upgrade.

name: repl-terminal-stable-ref
description: ReplTerminal uses a single empty-dep useEffect for xterm.js lifecycle; all async handlers (doEval, doCompletions) are defined inside the effect and access current repl/clientId/sessionId via a replRef updated on every render. Avoids re-creating the terminal on prop changes while keeping handlers current.

name: sparkline-freeze-pattern
description: ByteSparkline freeze in time travel: use frozenByteTimeline state + byteMetricsSamplesRef to snapshot byte samples at pause time; mirrors pausedInspector pattern (freeze on mode→paused, refresh on window load complete, clear on mode→live); TimelinePanel receives frozen vs live via ternary on timeTravelControls.mode.

content: studio-repl-package: @bt-studio/studio-repl holds all NaCl/HKDF deps (tweetnacl, @noble/hashes). Core has zero runtime deps. ReplPlugin + all crypto live in studio-repl; core/plugins exposes only pure-JS utilities and type interfaces. DEMO_SERVER_KEYPAIR is a fixed 32-byte seed keypair exported from studio-repl for demo mode.

name: plugin-message-payload-optional
description: PluginMessage payload optional: OutboundMessage and InboundMessage PluginMessage variants use payload?: unknown (not payload: unknown) so TypeScript types align with Zod z.unknown() which infers as optional. The compile-time assertions in studio-common/core-schemas.ts catch this mismatch.

name: zod-unknown-optional
description: z.unknown() in Zod objects infers as optional (payload?: unknown) because undefined extends unknown — TypeScript types must use payload?: unknown to match Zod schema; IsExact compile checks will fail otherwise

name: tick-reentracy-guard
description: BehaviourTree.tick() checks runtime.isTickRunning at entry and throws 'Re-entrant tick detected' to prevent duplicate tickIds and double-increment bugs from re-entrant calls

content: Composite runtime empty-children throw messages use this.displayName (not this.name) to avoid double-space in unnamed nodes — consistent with Composite.validate()

name: localStorage-node22-test-fix
description: Node 22+ ships a partial Web Storage stub missing removeItem/getItem/setItem. Fix: in vitest setup, check typeof localStorage.removeItem !== function and replace globalThis.localStorage with a full in-memory Storage implementation using Object.defineProperty with writable+configurable.

name: drag-listener-unmount-cleanup
description: WindowRangeTrimmer and similar components add native pointermove/pointerup listeners to window inside pointerdown handlers. Track cleanup functions in activeDragCleanupRef (useRef array); run all cleanups in useEffect return (empty deps) to prevent listener leaks when component unmounts mid-drag.

name: useStudioControls-tt-reset-on-selection
description: ttWindowTicks/ttWindowLoading/windowMaxTicks must be cleared in a useEffect with [selection?.clientId, selection?.sessionId, selection?.treeId] deps. Without this, stale TT window ticks from the previous tree remain visible when the user switches trees.

name: useInspector-dead-reindex-effect
description: The re-index useEffect (prevTreeRef check) was dead code: useMemo(deps=[tree,...]) already sets prevTreeRef.current=tree before the effect fires, making the condition always false. Removed both the effect and prevTreeRef.

name: tcp-stale-socket-cleanup
description: TCP transport stale socket: TcpBinaryTransport and TcpStringTransport must set this.socket=null in the onError handler of open() to prevent stale socket refs after failed connections. Without this, subsequent API calls (send/onMessage/onError/onClose) won't throw as expected.

name: base64url-invalid-length
description: base64urlDecode: length % 4 === 1 is inherently invalid base64url (cannot represent valid byte sequences). Must throw rather than produce '===' padding which gives corrupt output. Valid base64url strings only have length % 4 in {0, 2, 3}.

content: studio-common vitest.config.ts requires resolve.alias mapping @bt-studio/core to packages/core/src/index.ts because core has no dist in worktrees. Without this alias, core-schemas.ts imports of runtime values (MessageType, StudioCommandType) fail.

objectLiteralTypeAssertions: 'never' forbids ALL object literal assertions including double-cast (as unknown as T) and as never. Workaround: split into two statements: 'const _x: unknown = {}; const x = _x as T;' — the second assertion is not on an object literal.

name: repl-tab-view
description: REPL terminal is a first-class view tab in App.tsx (AppView='debugger'|'repl'), switching via tab bar with terminal icon; no bottom-drawer; ReplTerminal fills full height when active

name: repl-demo-keypair
description: useRepl seeds localStorage with DEMO_PRIVATE_KEY (Uint8Array 31 zeros + 0x01) on first load via loadDefaultKeyPair(); users can paste hex-64 or base64url private key via importPrivateKeyFromString(); ReplKeyPair now exports privateKeyB64 alongside publicKeyB64

name: transport-base-class
description: TcpTransportBase, WsNodeTransportBase, WsBrowserTransportBase hold shared open/close/onError/onClose lifecycle. Concrete variants (Binary/String) only implement send() and onMessage(). Base classes export from their respective index.ts alongside concrete classes.

name: knex-withTransaction-cast
description: knex-withTransaction-cast-pattern: withTransaction(qb: Knex.QueryBuilder): Knex.QueryBuilder (untyped) avoids TypeScript overload resolution failures with chained queries. Cast result at await site: (await this.withTransaction(query)) as DbRow[]. Typed generics like withTransaction<T extends {}> break on .first()/.count() chains due to knex type complexity.

name: setPointerCapture-jsdom-optional-chain
description: setPointerCapture?.(pointerId) must use optional chaining even on typed EventTarget because jsdom doesn't implement setPointerCapture. Without ?., tests throw 'setPointerCapture is not a function'. Add eslint-disable-next-line no-unnecessary-condition above the call.

content: Use error.toString() not error.message when displaying errors to preserve the 'Error: ' prefix. error.toString() = 'Error: Boom', error.message = 'Boom'. AsyncAction.getDisplayState() uses toString() for the error field.

REPL encryption is server-mediated, NOT E2E: ReplBroker holds serverSecretKey, decrypts agent handshake, derives session keys. Server has plaintext access to all eval commands/results. UI crypto code in use-repl.ts (openHandshake, establishSession, encrypted eval path) is dead — establishSession never called from ReplTerminal.tsx, tRPC schema only accepts plaintext code field (no encryptedPayload). Original design intent per comments was E2E (agent encrypts to UI keypair, server relays blindly) but implementation is server-mediated. DEMO_SERVER_KEYPAIR seed is shared across server, UI, and demo agent.

ReplPluginConfig rename: serverPublicKey→uiPublicKey (E2E crypto). Deprecated serverPublicKey kept as optional union branch for migration. DEMO_SERVER_KEYPAIR renamed to DEMO_UI_KEYPAIR in studio-plugins, re-exported as deprecated alias. getRandomBytes() throws instead of Math.random fallback in both studio-plugins and core.

studio-mcp package: McpServer (not deprecated Server) with registerTool() using zod inputSchema for typed args. StdioServerTransport for Claude Desktop integration. vitest.config.ts needs resolve.alias for @bt-studio/studio-plugins → ../studio-plugins/src/index.ts (no dist build required for tests).

name: studio-mcp-session-manager
description: studio-mcp SessionManager: Map<'clientId:sessionId', ReplClient> with ensureHandshake() (lazy init) and rehandshake() (force re-init). Tools call ensureHandshake on decrypt failure, then rehandshake then retry once. Shared private key across all sessions.

name: clear-command-regex
description: REPL clear command regex must be /^clear(\(\))?$/ — outer parens wrap the whole '()' group as optional unit, matching both 'clear' and 'clear()'. The broken form /^clear\(\)?$/ only makes ')' optional.

content: eslint-disable for non-existent rules (like react/no-danger when no react plugin is configured) must be removed — ESLint treats unknown rule disables as errors themselves

DomainEventDispatcherInterface on() method returns () => void (unsubscribe fn). Inline object literal mocks must return () => () => {} (not () => {}). Also, async mock methods returning Promise<void> should use () => Promise.resolve() to avoid require-await lint errors.

TESTING: Always use real dependencies instead of mocks/fakes. Follow Vladimir Khorikov's 'Unit Testing: Principles, Practices, and Patterns'. Use real classes (e.g. real EventDispatcher with null eventStore, not fake objects satisfying the interface). Only use test doubles at true system boundaries (external HTTP APIs, unavailable databases). Mocks hide real behavior, create false confidence, and couple tests to implementation. This is a hard project rule.

npm publish config for @bt-studio packages: publishConfig(access:public), repository(type:git, url, directory), homepage pointing to GitHub tree. workspace:* and workspace:^ in dependencies/peerDependencies replaced with ^0.1.0 for publish-readiness. devDependencies workspace refs left as-is.
